body:
This team is getting stronger by the day | Friend --

This is a big day for our team -- this morning, Bernie Sanders endorsed Hillary for president.

He joins President Obama, Vice President Biden, Elizabeth Warren, and millions of people all over America -- including you.

We’re stronger together. And right now, Hillary needs this whole team to fight for her harder than ever before, because we’re just six days out from the Republican convention -- and Donald Trump could announce his running mate even sooner than that.

We are about to see absolutely unprecedented attacks against Hillary, this team, women, Muslims, immigrants, Mexican Americans, people with disabilities, LGBT people, the middle class, and the values we share as Americans.

I’m so grateful Bernie stepped up as part of this team today -- we need him fighting with us. We need you, too.

**If you’re ready to stand with Hillary to fight back against Republican attacks and come out even stronger, chip in with just $1 today -- let’s show the world how strong our team really is:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjU5NjY2NTgS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxNzg1NjA5S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you,

Robby

Robby Mook
Campaign Manager
Hillary for America

|

| **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
** **Media Contact:**
Ethan Latour
ethan@philscott.org
802-595-3776
**Phil Scott Addresses Opponent’s Misleading Ads in New Video**
**_Scott calls on Vermonters to join his effort to "make political campaigns an example for our children...”_** Middlesex, Vt. – In contrast to the misleading negative attacks of his primary opponent, Phil Scott today released a new 60-second web video reiterating his core principles and urging Vermonters to join his campaign to “make Vermont more affordable, the economy stronger and political campaigns an example for our children.”

Scott opens the video by briefly addressing the tactics of his opponent, noting “what might pass for business as usual on Wall Street has never been in line with Vermont values.” He goes on to say, “While my opponent’s misleading ads are disappointing, Vermonters know the truth.”

“I’m the candidate who knows what it’s like to live paycheck-to-paycheck while raising a family and working hard to grow a business and create jobs -- right here in Vermont,” Scott continues.

“I’m proud of my record as a public servant and as a small business owner ... I’ve always believed in being honest, living by the Golden Rule and treating all people with dignity and respect. And I’m always going to stand by those principles,” Scott added.

Scott concludes by saying, “If you want to make Vermont more affordable, the economy stronger and political campaigns an example for our children – join us. And we’ll do it the Vermont way.”

Watch the full video here:

| | [](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U1swrSa04gY)
---

**ABOUT PHIL SCOTT:**
As the only Republican elected to statewide office, Phil Scott is a proven voice of reason in Montpelier. He is running for Governor to strengthen the economy; make Vermont more affordable for businesses and families; restore faith and trust in state government; and make political campaigns an example for our children.

disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF COLE
body:

| Friend --
Bill Cole sat down for an in-studio interview with the folks at _Panhandle Live_ in Martinsburg last Friday. A key element of his plan to make ** _West Virginia Proud and West Virginia Strong_** is to improve the results in our education system. Eighty years of failed leadership has left our state 48th in both reading and math among eighth graders. We must demand a better product for our children. **The Bill Cole Plan to Demand Excellence in Education**
* We must return to local control of our schools. Let teachers teach and principals manage.

* Break up the “education bureaucracy” in Charleston where there are currently 600 full-time positions.

* Institute a _Read by Third Grade West Virginia_ initiative.

* Stop Washington from telling us what to do, and create standards that work for West Virginia.

* Invest in better technology in our classrooms with one-to-one tablets.

* Use the savings from modernizing the bureaucracy in Charleston to give teachers the pay raise they deserve.

You can listen to the full segment [here](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fsoundcloud.com%2Fkent-gates%2Fbill-cole-talks-education-reform&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=education_refor&n=1). subject: Filling the potholes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gregg for Indiana
body:
Have you driven past a pothole lately? Or worse yet, driven over one?
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/5107522438305286144/-3412462379281807360?utm_campaign=adv_infras_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160713advinfrasgqfuljg&refcode=em16160713advinfrasgqfuljg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)
---
Peter, Have you driven past a pothole lately? Or worse yet, driven over one? That pothole isn't just a tire-eating bump in the road -- it's a sign of a bigger threat to our economy. For too long, Governor Pence and state legislators have refused to invest the money needed to build and repair the infrastructure Indiana's future demands. And it's time to do something about it. **[It's time to fix our crumbling roads and invest in Indiana's future. Sign my petition if you agree.](https://act.myngp.com/el/5107522438305286144/-3412462379281807360?utm_campaign=adv_infras_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160713advinfrasgqfuljg&refcode=em16160713advinfrasgqfuljg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)** Today, my running mate Christina Hale and I released a comprehensive, fiscally responsible plan to invest $3.2 billion in Indiana's infrastructure over the next 10 years -- without raising taxes. Our proposal not only funds repairs and improvements to our roads and bridges -- it also invests dormant state money into updating our outdated ports, airports, and railroads. Under our plan, we'll create a Hoosier State Infrastructure Bank to fund local projects, establish an infrastructure roundtable with private sector exports, provide transparency through an open and searchable infrastructure database, return control and resources to local government, and address Indiana's long-term water needs. Together, we can implement this ambitious plan and create an estimated 54,600 new jobs for Hoosiers. But we need your help to show our state's leaders that it's time for new investment in infrastructure so we can get moving on day one. **[Add your name to tell our state's leaders: "It's time to fix our crumbling roads and invest in Indiana's future!"](https://act.myngp.com/el/5107522438305286144/-3412462379281807360?utm_campaign=adv_infras_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&ms=em16160713advinfrasgqfuljg&refcode=em16160713advinfrasgqfuljg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)** Thanks for your support, John

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
body:
Peter,
I have some good news and some bad news.

The good news is that the public poll (http://moscout.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/07/MOScout-Weekly-Poll-July-8.pdf?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=37fb3500e4-07.14.16+Parkville+ETO&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-37fb3500e4-&utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=37fb3500e4-07.14.16+Parkville+ETO&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-37fb3500e4-99411629) released earlier this week shows Eric in the lead for the first time. Eric stands at 29%, at least 7% higher than the career politicians. Missourians all over the state are responding to Eric’s outsider conservative message. And everywhere we go, we feel the momentum growing.

The bad news is this means the opposition is becoming increasingly desperate. The professional politicians and special interests are coming at us from every angle. And we’ll see these attacks on full display at tonight's debate in St. Louis that will be broadcast statewide.

You can show your support for Eric by signing up to RSVP here (https://www.eventbrite.com/myevent?eid=26447282534&utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=37fb3500e4-07.14.16+Parkville+ETO&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-37fb3500e4-&utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=37fb3500e4-07.14.16+Parkville+ETO&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-37fb3500e4-99411629) . If you can't make it, you can watch live at this link (http://www.kfvs12.com/?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=37fb3500e4-07.14.16+Parkville+ETO&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-37fb3500e4-&utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=37fb3500e4-07.14.16+Parkville+ETO&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-37fb3500e4-99411629) .

Expect these desperate career politicians to gang up on Eric the entire debate. They'll attack his character, his military record, his family, the Mission Continues, and anything else they can spin to help save them. They think Eric is a threat to their stranglehold on power and will put an end to politics as usual.

And they’re right.
We’ve already seen incredibly shameless lies from our opponents. And just last week, John Brunner's former chief of staff admitted he was responsible for an anonymous video falsely attacking Eric's military service. Now Brunner's team is falsely attacking Eric with a SuperPAC under federal and state investigation. That's just how dirty these political insiders and career politicians really are.

But we aren't going to sit back and let them get away with it. Eric’s a fighter, and we're ready for what’s ahead. So we’re asking you to stand with us and have Eric's back at the debate tonight.
I look forward to seeing you there.

On to victory,

AC

Austin Chambers
Campaign Manager

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

4579 Laclede Avenue #138
St. Louis, Missouri 63108

Eric is a former member of the United States Navy having served four deployments overseas. Use of his military rank, titles and photographs in uniform does not imply an endorsement from the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

And everywhere we go, we feel the momentum growing. The bad news is this means the opposition is becoming increasingly desperate. The professional politicians and special interests are coming at us from every angle. And we’ll see these attacks on full display at tonight's debate in St. Louis that will be broadcast statewide. You can show your support for Eric by signing up to RSVP here () . If you can't make it, you can watch live at this link () . Expect these desperate career politicians to gang up on Eric the entire debate. They'll attack his character, his military record, his family, the Mission Continues, and anything else they can spin to help save them. They think Eric is a threat to their stranglehold on power and will put an end to politics as usual. And they’re right. We’ve already seen incredibly shameless lies from our opponents. And just last week, John Brunner's former chief of staff admitted he was responsible for an anonymous video falsely attacking Eric's military service. Now Brunner's team is falsely attacking Eric with a SuperPAC under federal and state investigation. That's just how dirty these political insiders and career politicians really are. But we aren't going to sit back and let them get away with it. Eric’s a fighter, and we're ready for what’s ahead. So we’re asking you to stand with us and have Eric's back at the debate tonight. I look forward to seeing you there. name: Rob Portman
subject: Great News: CARA Headed to the President's Desk!
body:
| Peter -- I have great news -- the Senate just voted to send my bipartisan legislation, the Comprehensive Addiction and Recovery Act (CARA), to the President's desk to be signed into law.

When it comes to Ohio's addiction epidemic, hope is on the way. CARA expands education and prevention efforts, helps law enforcement reverse overdoses to save lives, and expands treatment and recovery programs.

I’ve crisscrossed Ohio to learn what we can do to fight this epidemic. Last week in Dayton, I met with Sheriff Phil Plummer and announced the launch of the ‘Front Door Initiative’ that aims to get addicts back to work and get back in good graces with their families. In Toledo, I heard stories of recovery and what works and what doesn't. In Marion, I heard from a family dealing with unimaginable loss because of addiction. In Columbus, I visited Amethyst, Inc. and met with women in long-term recovery from drug addiction. In Cleveland, I saw compassionate care being given to babies who were born addicted because their mothers were using while pregnant. People all across our state are hurting because of addiction.

[](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DXM8fw1mBAPY&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=cara_update4&n=1)
Since I was elected to the Senate, I've focused on getting results for Ohio families, passing more than 45 bills into law. In addition to my work on combating Ohio's drug epidemic, we've passed legislation to combat human trafficking and level the playing field for Ohio companies competing with China.

Thanks for your support. All best,
Rob Portman [](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=cara_update4&n=2)[](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=cara_update4&n=3)[

](https://www.robportman.com/donate?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=cara_update4&n=4)
